Confidential · Trading Consultant Report · 2026

Olive Oil Mill
Investment Analysis

Industrial Production Line Assessment — Megano Mills Systems
Prepared by: digitalAIventures LLC Date: April 2026 Ref: MM-OL-2026-01 CONFIDENTIAL
01 · Executive Summary

Investment Overview

This report presents a comprehensive technical and financial assessment of three industrial olive oil production lines sourced from Megano Mills Systems, an Asian group manufacturer with demonstrated experience in centrifugal separation technology and CE-compliant machinery for the European market.

The analysis covers three capacity tiers — MM-250 (250 kg/h), MM-500 (500 kg/h), and MM-1000 (1,000 kg/h) — with full CIF pricing to Motril port, Granada, Spain. The evaluation includes technology assessment, full CAPEX breakdown, ROI projections, CE certification status, regulatory requirements for Spain, and a decision framework based on olive grove capacity.

Key Finding: The technology is sound and the prices are competitive compared to European manufacturers (typical Italian/Spanish equivalent lines cost 40–70% more). The critical open item is full-line CE certification, which must be contractually secured before order confirmation.
02 · Supplier Profile

About Megano Mills Systems

Megano Mills Systems operates as a multi-subsidiary industrial group, manufacturing centrifugal separation and food processing equipment. The group produces lines under multiple brand identities sharing the same engineering base and contact network — a common structure among Chinese manufacturing groups serving different market segments.

  • Experience supplying olive oil processing lines to Mediterranean markets (Spain, Morocco, Tunisia)
  • CE certification capability confirmed for complete extraction lines (Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC)
  • Modular design philosophy — each unit is independently interchangeable
  • All food-contact surfaces: 304 stainless steel
  • 18-month warranty from delivery date
  • On-site commissioning available (at buyer cost: flights + accommodation + USD 100/day)
  • 90 working-day lead time from confirmed order and 50% deposit
Note: Supplier identity is withheld from this report at the consultant's discretion. Full supplier dossier available upon request and signing of NDA.
03 · Technology & Process

How Virgin Olive Oil Is Produced

All three lines follow the standard continuous centrifugal extraction process, which is the global industry benchmark for EVOO production. The 8-step process is fully contained within the production line footprint.

Production line process flow — olive fruit to refined oil barrels
01
Defoliation
Belt conveyor with induced draft fan removes leaves and branches. Prevents chlorophyll contamination and oxidation.
02
Washing
Circulating water wash removes dust, sand, residual leaves. Water recycled via partition filtration system. Ratio: 2t water per 5t fruit.
03
Crushing
Hammer mill at 2,500–3,000 rpm with 6mm mesh converts whole olives to pulp. High-speed impact preserves polyphenols.
04
Malaxation (Fusion)
Horizontal ribbon mixer at 30–50 rpm for 30–50 min at ≤27°C (cold extraction certified). Double-jacket hot water maintains temperature uniformity. Oil droplets agglomerate for efficient separation.
05
Decanting
2-phase or 3-phase horizontal decanter centrifuge separates oil, water, and pomace at high speed. Vibration detection and speed tracking system for safe operation.
06
Purification
Vertical disc centrifuge at 6,600–8,000 rpm removes residual water from oil phase. 10–15% wash water added during this stage.
07
Storage
Centrifugally separated EVOO pumped to stainless steel storage tanks under nitrogen blanket at constant temperature. Oil foot periodically drained.
08
Filling & Packing
Oil dispensed to glass bottles with sealing, labelling, and packing. Optional nitrogen filling to extend shelf life (add-on module).
Main equipment components overview

MM-250 vs MM-500 vs MM-1000

Parameter MM-250 MM-500 MM-1000

Malaxer Units123
Containers1×20GP1×40HQ + 1×20GP2×40HQ
CIF Motril Price~€91,100 (conf.)€130,500€168,000
€ per kg/h capacity€364 (conf.)€261€168
Season Throughput*84,000 kg168,000 kg336,000 kg
Oil Yield (Picual, ~23%)14,700–16,800 L29,400–33,600 L58,800–67,200 L

* Season throughput calculated at 8h/day × 42 days (standard Andalucían harvest). MM-250 CIF price confirmed at USD 99,000 (€91,100). Supplier themselves recommend GLY-500 over GLY-250 for better cost-performance. Prices converted at USD/EUR 0.92.

⚠ Critical — Space Requirement Difference: The MM-250 is a compact, semi-mobile unit (~10 m²) that can fit inside an existing farm building, olive warehouse, or large garage with standard ceiling height. The MM-500 and MM-1000 require a dedicated industrial building of minimum 250–300 m². If the client does not already have a suitable structure, this represents a major additional investment not included in any of the equipment prices below.

Industrial Building Requirements (MM-500 / MM-1000)

The following building specifications are the minimum standard for a food-industry olive oil mill in Spain. The building is not included in any equipment price — the client must rent, build, or renovate an existing structure accordingly.

📐 Floor Areamin. 250–300 m²▼
Equipment footprint: 159 m² (19.55m × 8.13m). Add minimum 1m clear walkway on all sides for safety and maintenance access. Receiving area for olive trucks: additional 40–60 m². Pomace bin storage area adjacent to line exit. Recommended total operational floor area: 250–300 m².
📏 Ceiling Heightmin. 5 m clear▼
The inclined slag discharge conveyor reaches approximately 2.9m at its highest point. Add clearance for maintenance access above, plus structural elements. Minimum 5m clear internal height recommended. Standard agricultural buildings (3–3.5m) are NOT sufficient for MM-500/1000.
🏗 Floor Specificationindustrial epoxy, 15cm+ concrete▼
Required: reinforced concrete slab minimum 15–20cm thick (machinery loads). Food industry standard: epoxy resin coating, anti-slip, chemical-resistant, seamless finish. Drainage channels every 3–4m (stainless steel or epoxy-coated grating). Floor must slope minimum 1.5% toward drains. Smooth screed floors and agricultural concrete are NOT acceptable under RGSEAA food industry regulations.
🚛 Access & Doorsmin. 4m wide × 4.5m high▼
Minimum one industrial roller shutter door 4m wide × 4.5m high for truck access (olive delivery by tractor trailer). Separate personnel access door. Hardstand apron outside for truck maneuvering minimum 10m depth.
🌡 Temperature & Ventilationrequired by food regs▼
The production area must be enclosed and temperature-controllable. Harvest season (November–December) in Alpujarras: ambient 5–15°C, which is acceptable for malaxation. Industrial ventilation required to remove steam and odors from washing and decanting stages. Air handling unit recommended.
💧 Drainage & Waterfood industry spec▼
All floor drains must connect to a dedicated wastewater collection system (NOT municipal sewer without authorization). Stainless steel or epoxy-coated drainage channels preferred. Cold and hot water supply points throughout the production area. Separate toilet and handwashing facilities mandatory under food hygiene regulations.
💶 Building Cost Estimate€80K–250K depending on option▼
New construction (300 m² industrial nave, Andalucía): €150,000–250,000 including foundations, structure, epoxy floor, drainage, utilities. Renovation of existing agricultural building: €50,000–100,000 depending on current state. Rental of existing industrial unit near harvest location: €800–2,000/month. NOTE: This cost is entirely separate from and additional to all equipment prices in this report.

06 · ROI & Break-Even Analysis

Revenue projections are based on Andalucían conditions: 42-day harvest, 8h/day, mixed Picual/Arbequina varieties at average 17L oil per 100kg olives. Three wholesale EVOO price scenarios are modelled.

Important caveat: ROI figures assume the client has sufficient olives to run the line near full capacity during the entire harvest season. A line running at 30% capacity dramatically worsens payback. The right sizing question is: how many kilograms of olives does the client harvest per season?
Third-party milling income opportunity: If the client's own production doesn't fill the line, surplus capacity can be offered to neighboring olive farmers at €25–45 per 100kg milling service — a common practice in Andalucía that significantly improves ROI.
07 · CE Certification

Regulatory Compliance — Critical Item

⚠ Red Flag Resolved But Not Closed: The CE certificate provided by the supplier covers a phycocyanin (spirulina pigment) extraction line — a completely different product. This is NOT valid for the olive oil production line. We have confirmed with the supplier that CE certification for the complete olive oil line will be included in the formal contract.

What Is Required

For legal installation and operation in Spain (EU), the complete production line must carry CE marking under the Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC. This requires:

  • Technical documentation file per EN ISO 12100:2010 (risk assessment)
  • Electrical safety compliance per EN 60204-1:2018
  • Declaration of Conformity signed by manufacturer
  • CE marking affixed to all machines
  • Operating manuals in Spanish

Can the Line Be Certified?

Yes. The machinery uses standard components (centrifuges, conveyors, ribbon mixers) that individually comply with applicable harmonised standards. The risk is not certifiability — it is timeline and cost if left to after purchase.

Recommended Contract Language

"Full CE certification of the complete production line under EU Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC shall be included in the scope of supply. Delivery of CE Declaration of Conformity and technical documentation file in English and Spanish is a condition precedent to final payment release."

Estimated certification cost: €4,000–6,500 (included in CAPEX above). Timeline: typically 60–90 days, must be aligned with production lead time.

08 · Ancillaries & Infrastructure

What the Manufacturer Does NOT Supply

The quotes cover only the production line machinery. A complete operational mill requires significant additional investment — often underestimated by first-time buyers.

💧 Water Supply & Boiler System€3,500–8,000▼
The line requires continuous supply of hot water for malaxation jacket heating. A dedicated industrial boiler or heat exchanger must be sourced locally. In off-grid or rural Alpujarras conditions, water storage tanks and pressure pumps may also be needed. Connection to municipal supply must meet food-industry standards.
🛢 Oil Storage Tanks (Nitrogen)€8,000–25,000▼
EVOO must be stored in 304 stainless steel tanks under nitrogen blanket at controlled temperature (15–18°C) to prevent oxidation. Tank capacity should match 2–3 weeks of production. Insulation, N₂ supply system, and temperature monitoring required. Sizing: 2,000L tanks for MM-250, 5,000–10,000L for MM-500/1000.
🍾 Bottling Line€6,000–23,000▼
The production line ends at oil storage tanks. Bottling (fill, cork/cap, label, box) is a separate investment. Options: (1) Basic semi-automatic line €6,000–10,000; (2) + Nitrogen flushing module €4,000–8,000 extra for premium EVOO shelf life extension to 24 months; (3) Contract external bottler if volumes don't justify own line. Nitrogen bottling is strongly recommended for premium branded EVOO.
♻️ Alpechín Wastewater Management€5,000–18,000▼
Olive mill wastewater (alpechín) is legally classified as industrial effluent in Spain. It cannot be discharged to municipal sewers without prior authorization. Options: (1) Evaporation lagoon (most common in rural Andalucía) — requires land, earthworks, waterproof lining €5,000–12,000; (2) Discharge agreement with municipal WWTP — requires analysis, permits, per-volume fee; (3) Transport to licensed treatment facility — ongoing operational cost. This is frequently the biggest overlooked cost for new mills.
🌿 Pomace Handling€1,500–4,000 + logistics▼
Wet pomace (orujo húmedo) at 60%+ water content exits the decanter continuously. It must be collected in bins and either: (1) Transported to an orujera (pomace oil extractor) — they typically collect for free or small cost, as pomace has residual commercial value; (2) Composted on-site (requires space and management). Plan for adequate bin storage adjacent to the line.
⚡ Electrical Infrastructure€4,000–16,000▼
The line requires 3-phase 380V industrial power. Peak draw is 92kW (MM-500/1000), equivalent to a small industrial facility. Rural Alpujarras locations may require grid upgrade or transformer installation (coordinate with Endesa). A dedicated electrical panel, emergency stop circuits, and earthing system are required by Spanish industrial regulations.
🔬 Quality Laboratory€3,000–5,000▼
For EVOO certification and commercial sale in Spain, the producer must be able to demonstrate: free acidity (≤0.8%), peroxide value (≤20 mEq/kg), and organoleptic characteristics. Minimum lab: digital acidity titration kit, peroxide measurement, UV spectrophotometer. For full polyphenol content claims (UHP/HPP marketing), additional HPLC analysis via accredited lab is required.
🤖 Automation Upgrades€3,000–15,000 (post-install)▼
The standard control cabinet uses PLC architecture. Post-installation upgrades feasible: flow meters, automated valve control, temperature sensors, SCADA monitoring dashboard. NOT easily retrofittable: malaxation temperature precision modules (must be specified at order), CIP (clean-in-place) automated washing systems. Update (confirmed): PLC is Siemens, supporting Modbus / Profibus / EtherNet IP — the gold standard in Spanish industrial installations. Every local integrator works with it. This significantly improves post-installation automation potential.
Malaxation combination box — double-jacket heated, ribbon mixer drive
Decanter centrifuge — LWS350 series, vibration detection, speed tracking
09 · Permits & Authorizations

Spain — Regulatory Requirements

Operating an industrial olive oil mill in Spain requires a set of municipal, regional, and national authorizations. Timeline from application to first legal production: typically 6–18 months depending on municipality.

🏛
Licencia de Actividad / Licencia Ambiental
Municipal permit for industrial activity. Required before installation. Requires technical project by licensed architect or industrial engineer. In Andalucía: Ley 7/2007 GICA governs environmental authorization for activities with significant impact.
🍶
RGSEAA — Registro General Sanitario de Empresas Alimentarias y Alimentos
Mandatory food industry registration with AESAN (Spanish Food Safety Agency). Required to legally produce and commercialize olive oil. Online application via AESAN portal.
🌱
Autorización Ambiental / Notificación Ambiental
Environmental authorization from Junta de Andalucía. Required due to alpechín generation (industrial wastewater classified as contaminating). Must include wastewater management plan.
💧
Autorización de Vertidos
Authorization for wastewater discharge (Confederación Hidrográfica del Guadalquivir if applicable). Even for evaporation lagoons, prior notification and approval is required in most municipalities.
⚡
🔌
Autorización de Instalación Eléctrica Industrial
Registration of industrial electrical installation with the Delegación Provincial de Industria (Junta de Andalucía). Requires inspection by authorized OCA.
📦
Importación de Maquinaria — DUA
Customs declaration (Documento Único Administrativo) for machinery import from China. Managed by customs agent (agente de aduanas). Import VAT recoverable. Possible anti-dumping checks for food processing machinery — verify HS code classification.
🏷
Denominación de Origen / IGP (Optional)
For premium positioning, registration with DO Sierra de Segura, DO Sierra Mágina, or other Andalucían PDO/PGI is possible if location and varieties qualify. Significantly increases commercial value.
Timeline advice: Begin permit applications before ordering machinery. The 90-day production lead time is shorter than the typical permit timeline. Starting both processes in parallel is essential to avoid costly delays.
10 · Model Analysis

Pros & Cons Per Configuration

Pros

  • Lowest entry investment (~€166K total)
  • Minimal floor space — 10 m² only
  • Ideal for 50,000–100,000 kg/season farms
  • Lower electrical infrastructure cost
  • Easiest to permit (smaller environmental footprint)
  • Run 2 units for redundancy later
  • Lower risk if harvest varies year to year

Cons

  • Worst €/kg-capacity ratio (€340/kg-h)
  • Only 84,000 kg/season capacity
  • Cannot scale without adding full unit
  • Less attractive for third-party milling service
  • Higher labor cost per liter produced

Pros

  • Best balance of cost vs capacity
  • 168,000 kg/season — suits medium estates
  • Good third-party milling potential
  • Modular — 1 extra malaxer upgrades to 1000
  • One 40HQ + one 20GP container
  • Strong ROI at €8/L scenario (1.4yr payback)

Cons

  • 92kW power demand — grid upgrade likely
  • ~160 m² floor area required
  • Higher wastewater management costs
  • More complex permit process
  • Higher ancillary investment

Pros

  • Best €/kg-capacity efficiency (€168/kg-h)
  • 336,000 kg/season — commercial scale
  • Strong case as community/cooperative mill
  • Best payback at high utilization (<1 year)
  • Premium for third-party milling service

Cons

  • Requires large olive supply to justify
  • Highest total CAPEX (~€372K)
  • Most complex permits and environmental impact
  • 2×40HQ containers — higher transport cost
  • Significant electrical infrastructure required
  • Full-line CE must be rigorously enforced in contract
11 · Decision Framework

Which Line Is Right?

The single most important factor is seasonal olive volume. The right machine is the one that runs at 70–100% capacity during the harvest, not the cheapest or most powerful.

≤ 80,000 kg/season
→ MM-250 or defer decision
At this volume the MM-250 is already oversized for half the season. Consider starting with contract milling at an existing almazara before investing. If the client is committed to own mill: MM-250 is the correct choice.
80,000–180,000 kg/season
→ MM-500 recommended
Sweet spot for the MM-500. Sufficient volume to run the line near capacity. Good platform for third-party milling to fill gaps. Best balance of efficiency and manageability.
180,000–340,000 kg/season
→ MM-1000 or MM-500 + scale plan
At these volumes the MM-1000 delivers clear efficiency advantages. Alternatively: two MM-500 units offer redundancy at similar total cost. Cooperative model with neighboring farms makes compelling business case.
Unknown / first year
→ MM-500 as safe default
If harvest volume is uncertain, the MM-500 offers the most flexibility: large enough to run a professional operation, modular enough to upgrade with one malaxer later, strong enough for third-party milling income to cover fixed costs.
GLY-250 class process diagram — from fresh fruit input to finished oil
12 · Next Steps

Action Plan

A
Confirm olive volume
Client must provide estimated kg of olives per season (own production + any third-party commitments). This determines the correct line size.
B
Site assessment
Verify: available floor area, 3-phase power availability (and upgrade cost), water supply, and wastewater options for the intended installation location.
C
Begin permit process
Engage a local architect or industrial engineer to prepare the licencia de actividad project. This can run in parallel with equipment ordering.
D
Negotiate contract with CE clause
Once line size confirmed, formal purchase contract must include: full-line CE certification as condition precedent to final payment, technical manuals in Spanish, and agreed commission date.
E
Arrange import financing
50% deposit (advance payment) due at order. Balance before shipment. Plan for: import customs duty, transport to site, and cash flow for installation period before first revenue.
F
Plan ancillary procurement
Order boiler, storage tanks, electrical works, and bottling line in advance. These local items have their own lead times and should not be left until machinery arrives.
Consultant availability: digitalAIventures LLC can assist with supplier negotiation, contract review, CE certification coordination, and import logistics management. Contact for further engagement terms.

Confidencial · Informe Consultor Comercial · 2026

Almazara Industrial
Análisis de Inversión

Evaluación de Líneas de Producción de Aceite de Oliva — Megano Mills Systems
Elaborado por: digitalAIventures LLC Fecha: Abril 2026 Ref: MM-OL-2026-01 CONFIDENCIAL
01 · Resumen Ejecutivo

Visión General de la Inversión

Este informe presenta una evaluación técnica y financiera completa de tres líneas de producción industrial de aceite de oliva virgen extra, suministradas por Megano Mills Systems, un fabricante asiático con experiencia demostrada en tecnología de separación centrífuga y maquinaria con certificación CE para el mercado europeo.

El análisis cubre tres niveles de capacidad: MM-250 (250 kg/h), MM-500 (500 kg/h) y MM-1000 (1.000 kg/h), con precios CIF completos al puerto de Motril, Granada. La evaluación incluye análisis tecnológico, desglose completo de CAPEX, proyecciones de ROI, situación de certificación CE, requisitos regulatorios en España y marco de decisión según la capacidad del olivar.

Conclusión clave: La tecnología es sólida y los precios son competitivos frente a fabricantes europeos (líneas equivalentes italianas/españolas cuestan un 40–70% más). El punto crítico pendiente es la certificación CE de la línea completa, que debe quedar contractualmente garantizada antes de confirmar el pedido.
02 · Comparativa de Líneas

MM-250 vs MM-500 vs MM-1000

ParámetroMM-250MM-500MM-1000
Capacidad250 kg/h500 kg/h1.000 kg/h
Potencia instalada21 kW92 kW92 kW + malaxador
Superficie necesaria~10 m²~160 m²~160 m²
Precio CIF Motril~€91.100 (conf.)€130.500€168.000
€ por kg/h capacidad€364 (conf.)€261€168
Throughput campaña*84.000 kg168.000 kg336.000 kg
Rendimiento aceite (Picual ~23%)14.700–16.800 L29.400–33.600 L58.800–67.200 L
Rendimiento aceite (Arbequina ~20%)11.760–14.280 L23.520–28.560 L47.040–57.120 L

* Calculado a 8h/día × 42 días (campaña andaluza estándar). Precio CIF MM-250 estimado (pendiente cotización oficial). Conversión USD/EUR 0,92.

⚠ Diferencia crítica de espacio: El MM-250 es una unidad compacta semimóvil (~10 m²) que puede instalarse en una nave agrícola existente, almacén de aceitunas o garaje amplio con altura libre estándar. El MM-500 y MM-1000 requieren un edificio industrial dedicado de mínimo 250–300 m² con altura libre mínima de 5 m, solera epoxi industrial y drenajes conforme a normativa alimentaria. El edificio NO está incluido en ningún precio de equipo — el cliente deberá alquilar, construir o reformar una instalación existente. Coste estimado de construcción nueva: €150.000–250.000. Reforma de nave existente: €50.000–100.000.

04 · ROI y Rentabilidad

Retorno de la Inversión

Escenario precio AOVEMM-250MM-500MM-1000
Conservador — €5/L a granel€71.400€142.800€285.600
Medio — €8/L premium granel€114.240€228.480€456.960
Premium — €12/L AOVE etiquetado€171.360€342.720€685.440

Período de Recuperación Simple (a €8/L)

MM-250
1,7 años
Neto €100.740/campaña
CAPEX €166K
MM-500
1,4 años
Neto €200.980/campaña
CAPEX €278K
MM-1000
0,9 años
Neto €417.560/campaña
CAPEX €372K
Advertencia: El ROI supone que el cliente tiene suficiente volumen de aceituna para operar cerca de la capacidad máxima durante toda la campaña. Una línea al 30% de capacidad empeora dramáticamente el retorno.
Maquila: Si la producción propia no llena la línea, la capacidad sobrante puede ofrecerse a olivareros vecinos a €25–45 por 100 kg de aceituna — práctica habitual en Andalucía que mejora significativamente el ROI.
05 · Autorizaciones y Permisos

Trámites Necesarios en España

🏛
Licencia de Actividad / Ambiental (Ayuntamiento)
Imprescindible antes de instalar. Requiere proyecto técnico de arquitecto o ingeniero industrial. Ley 7/2007 GICA en Andalucía.
🍶
Registro RGSEAA (AESAN)
Registro sanitario obligatorio para producir y comercializar aceite de oliva en España. Solicitud online en portal AESAN.
🌱
Autorización Ambiental (Junta de Andalucía)
Requerida por la generación de alpechín (efluente industrial). Debe incluir plan de gestión de aguas residuales.
💧
Autorización de Vertidos
Incluso para balsas de evaporación, se requiere notificación y aprobación previa en la mayoría de municipios.
⚡
Instalación Eléctrica Industrial (Delegación de Industria)
Registro de la instalación eléctrica industrial. Inspección por OCA autorizada.
📦
Importación — DUA (Aduana)
Gestión por agente de aduanas. IVA de importación recuperable. Verificar clasificación arancelaria correcta para evitar contingencias anti-dumping.
Consejo clave: Iniciar los trámites de permisos antes de encargar la maquinaria. Los 90 días de plazo de fabricación son inferiores al tiempo habitual de tramitación de permisos. Ambos procesos deben correr en paralelo.
06 · Qué NO Incluye el Fabricante

Inversiones Adicionales Necesarias

💧 Sistema de agua caliente / Caldera€3.500–8.000▼
La línea requiere suministro continuo de agua caliente para la camisa de temperatura del malaxador. Caldera industrial o intercambiador de calor. En zonas rurales: depósito de acumulación + grupo de presión necesarios.
🛢 Depósitos de aceite en inerte (N₂)€8.000–25.000▼
El AOVE debe almacenarse en depósitos de acero inoxidable 304 bajo atmósfera de nitrógeno a 15–18°C. Dimensionar según producción diaria × días hasta embotellado. Incluir aislamiento, sistema N₂ y monitorización de temperatura.
🍾 Línea de Embotellado (± nitrógeno)€6.000–23.000▼
La línea acaba en el depósito de aceite. El embotellado (llenado, taponado, etiquetado, encajado) es inversión aparte. Opciones: (1) Línea semi-automática básica €6.000–10.000; (2) + módulo de inertización con N₂ +€4.000–8.000 para AOVE premium (vida útil 24 meses); (3) Envasado en instalación externa si los volúmenes no justifican línea propia.
♻️ Gestión de Alpechín€5.000–18.000▼
El alpechín (aguas de vegetación) está clasificado como efluente industrial en España y no puede verterse a la red municipal sin autorización. Opciones: (1) Balsa de evaporación — la más habitual en Andalucía rural, requiere obra civil y lámina impermeabilizante; (2) Acuerdo de descarga con EDAR municipal; (3) Transporte a gestor autorizado (coste operativo recurrente).
🌿 Gestión del Orujo Húmedo€1.500–4.000▼
El orujo húmedo (>60% humedad) sale del decánter de forma continua. Debe acumularse en tolvas o contenedores y transferirse a una orujera (que habitualmente lo recoge gratuitamente dado su valor residual en extracción de aceite de orujo).
⚡ Infraestructura Eléctrica€4.000–16.000▼
Se requiere corriente trifásica 380V. Pico de 92 kW (MM-500/1000). En zonas rurales de las Alpujarras puede ser necesario ampliar la acometida o instalar transformador (coordinar con Endesa). Cuadro eléctrico, paradas de emergencia y toma de tierra conforme a reglamento industrial español.
🔬 Laboratorio de Calidad€3.000–5.000▼
Para comercializar AOVE en España: acidez libre (≤0,8%), índice de peróxidos (≤20 mEq/kg) y análisis organoléptico. Equipamiento mínimo: kit de titulación digital, medidor de peróxidos, espectrofotómetro UV. Para certificaciones UHP/HPP (polifenoles): análisis HPLC en laboratorio acreditado externo.
07 · Marco de Decisión

¿Qué Línea Es la Correcta?

≤ 80.000 kg/campaña
→ MM-250 o valorar maquila externa primero
Con este volumen el MM-250 ya estaría sobredimensionado parte de la campaña. Si el cliente está decidido a tener almazara propia: el MM-250 es la opción correcta.
80.000–180.000 kg/campaña
→ MM-500 recomendado
Punto óptimo para el MM-500. Volumen suficiente para operar cerca del 100% de capacidad. Buen potencial para ofrecer maquila a terceros y rellenar huecos. Mejor equilibrio eficiencia/gestión.
180.000–340.000 kg/campaña
→ MM-1000 o modelo cooperativo
A estos volúmenes el MM-1000 ofrece ventajas claras de eficiencia. Un modelo cooperativo con olivareros vecinos hace muy atractivo el caso de negocio.
Volumen desconocido / primer año
→ MM-500 como opción segura por defecto
Si el volumen es incierto, el MM-500 ofrece más flexibilidad: suficientemente grande para operación profesional, actualizable con un malaxador extra, y con capacidad de maquila para cubrir costes fijos.
ANÁLISIS DE INVERSIÓN MEGANO MILLS · REF: MM-OL-2026-01
Elaborado por digitalAIventures LLC · Abril 2026 · CONFIDENCIAL

Riservato · Rapporto Consulente Commerciale · 2026

Frantoio Industriale
Analisi dell'Investimento

Valutazione Linee di Produzione Olio d'Oliva — Megano Mills Systems
Redatto da: digitalAIventures LLC Data: Aprile 2026 Rif: MM-OL-2026-01 RISERVATO
01 · Sintesi Esecutiva

Panoramica dell'Investimento

Questo rapporto presenta una valutazione tecnica e finanziaria completa di tre linee di produzione industriale di olio extravergine di oliva, fornite da Megano Mills Systems, un produttore asiatico con comprovata esperienza in tecnologia di separazione centrifuga e macchinari conformi CE per il mercato europeo.

L'analisi copre tre livelli di capacità — MM-250 (250 kg/h), MM-500 (500 kg/h) e MM-1000 (1.000 kg/h) — con prezzi CIF completi al porto di Motril, Granada, Spagna. La valutazione include analisi tecnologica, scomposizione completa del CAPEX, proiezioni ROI, stato della certificazione CE, requisiti normativi spagnoli e un framework decisionale basato sulla capacità produttiva dell'oliveto.

Conclusione chiave: La tecnologia è solida e i prezzi sono competitivi rispetto ai produttori europei (linee equivalenti italiane/spagnole costano il 40–70% in più). Il punto critico aperto è la certificazione CE della linea completa, che deve essere garantita contrattualmente prima della conferma dell'ordine.
02 · Confronto Linee

MM-250 vs MM-500 vs MM-1000

ParametroMM-250MM-500MM-1000
Capacità250 kg/h500 kg/h1.000 kg/h
Potenza installata21 kW92 kW92 kW + gramola
Superficie necessaria~10 m²~160 m²~160 m²
Prezzo CIF Motril~€91.100 (conf.)€130.500€168.000
€ per kg/h capacità€364 (conf.)€261€168
Produzione stagionale*84.000 kg168.000 kg336.000 kg
Resa olio (Picual ~23%)14.700–16.800 L29.400–33.600 L58.800–67.200 L
Resa olio (Arbequina ~20%)11.760–14.280 L23.520–28.560 L47.040–57.120 L

* Calcolato a 8h/giorno × 42 giorni. Prezzo CIF MM-250 stimato (preventivo ufficiale in attesa). Conversione USD/EUR 0,92.

⚠ Differenza critica di spazio: Il MM-250 è un'unità compatta semi-mobile (~10 m²) installabile in un capannone agricolo esistente. Il MM-500 e MM-1000 richiedono un capannone industriale dedicato di minimo 250–300 m², altezza libera minima 5 m, pavimento epossidico industriale e scarichi conformi alle normative alimentari. Il capannone NON è incluso nel prezzo delle apparecchiature. Costo stimato nuova costruzione: €150.000–250.000.

04 · ROI e Redditività

Ritorno sull'Investimento

Scenario prezzo EVOMM-250MM-500MM-1000
Conservativo — €5/L sfuso€71.400€142.800€285.600
Medio — €8/L premium sfuso€114.240€228.480€456.960

Periodo di Recupero Semplice (a €8/L)

MM-250
1,7 anni
Netto €100.740/stagione
CAPEX €166K

Netto €200.980/stagione

Attenzione: Il ROI presuppone che il cliente abbia volume sufficiente di olive per operare vicino alla piena capacità durante tutta la campagna. Una linea al 30% di capacità peggiora drasticamente il ritorno.
05 · Framework Decisionale

Quale Linea Scegliere?

≤ 80.000 kg/stagione
→ MM-250 o valutare prima la molitura conto terzi
Con questo volume, il MM-250 sarebbe già sovradimensionato per metà campagna. Se il cliente è determinato a investire: MM-250 è la scelta giusta.
80.000–180.000 kg/stagione
→ MM-500 raccomandato
Punto ottimale per il MM-500. Volume sufficiente per operare vicino al 100% di capacità. Buon potenziale per molitura conto terzi.
180.000–340.000 kg/stagione
→ MM-1000 o modello cooperativo
A questi volumi, il MM-1000 offre chiari vantaggi di efficienza. Un modello cooperativo con olivicoltori vicini rende il business case molto attrattivo.
Volume incerto / primo anno
→ MM-500 come scelta sicura predefinita
Se il volume è incerto, il MM-500 offre la massima flessibilità: abbastanza grande per un'operazione professionale, aggiornabile con una gramola aggiuntiva, con capacità di molitura conto terzi per coprire i costi fissi.

机密文件 · 贸易顾问报告 · 2026年

橄榄油工厂
投资分析报告

工业生产线评估 — Megano Mills Systems
编制：digitalAIventures LLC 日期：2026年4月 编号：MM-OL-2026-01 机密文件
01 · 执行摘要

投资概述

本报告对三条工业特级初榨橄榄油生产线进行了全面的技术与财务评估，设备由 Megano Mills Systems 提供——该集团制造商在离心分离技术和欧盟CE认证机械领域拥有丰富的实际经验。

本次分析涵盖三种产能规格：MM-250（250公斤/小时）、MM-500（500公斤/小时）和 MM-1000（1,000公斤/小时），均提供至西班牙格拉纳达省莫特里尔港的完整CIF报价。评估内容包括技术评估、完整资本支出（CAPEX）分解、投资回报率（ROI）预测、CE认证现状、西班牙监管要求以及基于橄榄园产能的决策框架。

核心结论：技术成熟，与欧洲同类制造商相比价格极具竞争力（意大利/西班牙同等生产线通常高出40–70%）。当前最关键的待解事项是完整生产线的CE认证，必须在确认订单前通过合同形式予以明确保障。
02 · 生产线对比

MM-250 vs MM-500 vs MM-1000

生产线工艺流程图 — 从橄榄鲜果到精制橄榄油桶
参数MM-250MM-500MM-1000
处理产能250公斤/小时500公斤/小时1,000公斤/小时
装机功率21千瓦92千瓦92千瓦 + 混合机
占地面积约10平方米约160平方米约160平方米
CIF莫特里尔港价格~€91,100 （已确认）€130,500€168,000
单位产能成本（€/公斤·小时）€364 （已确认）€261€168
季节处理量*84,000公斤168,000公斤336,000公斤
Picual品种出油量（含油率约23%）14,700–16,800升29,400–33,600升58,800–67,200升
Arbequina品种出油量（含油率约20%）11,760–14,280升23,520–28,560升47,040–57,120升
交货周期收到定金后90个工作日
质保期自交货之日起18个月

* 按每天8小时、采摘季42天计算。MM-250 CIF价格为估算值（待供应商提供正式报价）。汇率：1美元 ≈ 0.92欧元。

⚠ 关键差异——空间需求：MM-250为紧凑型半移动设备（约10平方米），可安装于现有农业建筑、橄榄仓库或普通车库内。MM-500和MM-1000则需要专用工业厂房，最小面积250–300平方米，最低净高5米，工业环氧地坪，符合食品卫生法规的排水系统。厂房费用不含在设备报价中，客户须自行解决（租赁、新建或改造现有建筑）。新建估价：€150,000–250,000；改造现有建筑：€50,000–100,000。

视频：生产线实际运行

在 YouTube 上观看
03 · 生产工艺

特级初榨橄榄油生产工艺（八步流程）

01
除叶分选
皮带输送机配合高速引风机，去除鲜果中混杂的叶片与枝条，防止叶绿素氧化影响油品质量。
02
清洗
循环水冲洗去除果面泥沙、灰尘和残余叶片。用水比例：2吨水处理5吨鲜果。清洗水循环利用。
03
破碎
锤式破碎机，转速2,500–3,000转/分钟，筛网孔径约6毫米，将完整橄榄果破碎为橄榄浆。
04
混合（马拉克萨）
卧式螺旋搅拌机，转速30–50转/分钟，搅拌时间30–50分钟，温度≤27°C（冷压认证）。双层水套保温，促进油滴聚结，提高出油率。
05
卧螺离心分离
卧螺卸料离心机高速分离油、水和果渣。配备专业振动检测和转速跟踪系统，确保安全可靠运行。
06
碟片分离纯化
立式高速碟片分离机，转速6,600–8,000转/分钟，进一步去除油相中残余水分，获得纯净特级初榨橄榄油。
07
储存
分离所得橄榄油泵入304不锈钢储罐，充氮密封，恒温储存，避光避热，定期排放底部沉积物。
08
灌装包装
橄榄油灌装入玻璃瓶，封盖、贴标、装箱。可选配氮气冲洗模块，延长优质特级初榨橄榄油货架期至24个月。
主要设备组成总览图
04 · 总投资（CAPEX）估算

完整投资成本分解

设备报价仅为起点。实际投资还须包括场地准备、配套设施、附属设备、认证费用及不可预见费用。以下为各配置的保守估算。

约€166K

05 · 投资回报率（ROI）

收益与回报分析

收益预测基于安达卢西亚地区实际条件：采摘季42天，每天8小时，混合Picual/Arbequina品种，平均出油率约17升/100公斤橄榄。针对三种特级初榨橄榄油批发价格情景进行建模分析。

价格情景MM-250MM-500MM-1000
保守情景 — €5/升（散装批发）€71,400€142,800€285,600
中等情景 — €8/升（优质散装）€114,240€228,480€456,960
高端情景 — €12/升（品牌特级初榨）€171,360€342,720€685,440

简单回收期（按€8/升计算）

MM-250
1.7年
净收益€100,740/季
总投资€166K
MM-500
1.4年
净收益€200,980/季
总投资€278K
MM-1000
0.9年
净收益€417,560/季
总投资€372K
重要提示：ROI数据基于客户在整个采摘季均能维持接近满负荷运转的前提。若实际产能利用率仅为30%，回收期将大幅延长。关键问题是：客户每个采摘季实际有多少公斤橄榄？
代加工收入机会：若自有橄榄无法填满生产线，剩余产能可向周边橄榄农户提供加工服务，收费约€25–45/100公斤，这是安达卢西亚地区的普遍做法，可显著改善投资回报。
06 · CE认证分析

合规认证——关键事项

⚠ 重要警示：供应商提供的CE证书所认证的产品为"藻蓝素完整提取线"（一种螺旋藻色素提取设备），与橄榄油生产线完全无关。该证书对本项目不具有法律效力。我们已与供应商确认，完整橄榄油生产线的CE认证将纳入正式合同范围。

在西班牙（欧盟）合法安装和运营，完整生产线须取得《机械指令2006/42/EC》框架下的CE标志，具体要求包括：符合EN ISO 12100:2010的风险评估技术文档、EN 60204-1:2018电气安全符合性、制造商签署的符合性声明、所有设备贴附CE标志，以及西班牙语操作手册。

建议合同条款（参考语言）："完整生产线依据欧盟机械指令2006/42/EC取得CE认证，为供货范围的组成部分。向买方交付英文及西班牙文版CE符合性声明和技术文档，是尾款支付的前提条件。"

预估认证费用：€4,000–6,500（已计入上述CAPEX）。所需时间：通常60–90天，须与生产周期协调一致。

07 · 制造商未提供的配套设施

采购方须自行解决的投资项目

💧 热水供应系统 / 锅炉€3,500–8,000▼
生产线马拉克萨混合机的水套加热需要持续热水供应，须配置工业锅炉或换热器。在安达卢西亚山区偏远地区，可能还需储水罐和增压泵，供水须达到食品工业卫生标准。
🛢 充氮不锈钢储油罐€8,000–25,000▼
特级初榨橄榄油须在304不锈钢罐中充氮密封，恒温（15–18°C）保存，防止氧化。储罐容量应匹配2–3周产量，须配备隔热层、氮气供应系统和温度监控装置。
🍾 灌装线（含/不含氮气充填）€6,000–23,000▼
生产线终点为储油罐，灌装（充填、封盖、贴标、装箱）需单独投资。选项：（1）半自动基础灌装线€6,000–10,000；（2）加配氮气冲洗模块+€4,000–8,000，优质特级初榨橄榄油货架期可延长至24个月；（3）若产量不足以支撑自建灌装线，可委托外部灌装合作商。
♻️ 橄榄磨坊废水（Alpechín）处理€5,000–18,000▼
橄榄加工废水（alpechín）在西班牙被列为工业废水，未经许可不得排入市政管网。处理方案：（1）蒸发塘——安达卢西亚农村最常见方式，需要土地、土建和防渗膜；（2）与市政污水处理厂签订排放协议；（3）委托有资质机构运输处理（需计入持续运营成本）。这往往是新建橄榄油厂最容易被忽视的重大成本项。
🌿 湿橄榄渣（Orujo）处理€1,500–4,000▼
卧螺离心机持续产出含水率超60%的湿橄榄渣。须在料仓中暂存后转运至橄榄渣加工厂（orujera），通常可免费回收，因为橄榄渣仍具有提取橄榄渣油的商业价值。
⚡ 工业电气基础设施€4,000–16,000▼
须配备三相380V工业电源。MM-500/1000峰值用电量92千瓦，相当于一座小型工业设施。安达卢西亚山区农村地区可能需要扩容供电容量或安装变压器（需与Endesa协调）。须配置专用电气控制盘、紧急停机回路和接地系统，符合西班牙工业电气规程。
🔬 质量检验实验室€3,000–5,000▼
在西班牙合法销售特级初榨橄榄油，须能证明：游离酸度（≤0.8%）、过氧化值（≤20毫当量/千克）和感官特性指标合格。最低配置：数字酸度滴定装置、过氧化值测定仪、紫外分光光度计。若计划进行多酚含量（UHP/HPP）宣传，需委托具备资质的外部实验室进行HPLC检测。
08 · 西班牙监管许可

在西班牙建设经营橄榄油厂所需许可

🏛
经营活动许可证 / 环境许可证（市政府）
安装前的必备许可。须由持证建筑师或工业工程师编制技术项目方案。安达卢西亚地区适用2007年第7号法律（GICA）。
🍶
RGSEAA食品企业卫生登记（AESAN）
在西班牙生产和销售橄榄油的强制性食品卫生注册。通过AESAN官网在线申请。
🌱
环境授权（安达卢西亚自治区政府）
因产生橄榄磨坊废水（工业废水）而必须申请。须附废水管理方案。
💧
废水排放许可
即使建设蒸发塘，大多数市镇也要求事先通知并获得批准。
⚡
工业电气安装许可（工业省级代表处）
须向安达卢西亚自治区工业厅办理工业电气安装登记，并经授权合格评定机构（OCA）检验。
📦
机械进口——单一行政文件（DUA）
由报关行办理。进口增值税可抵扣回收。建议提前核实食品加工机械的正确商品编码，避免反倾销核查风险。
时间节点建议：在订购设备之前启动许可证申请程序。设备90天生产周期通常短于许可证办理所需时间。两项工作须并行推进，避免造成高昂的等待损失。
09 · 决策框架

如何选择合适的生产线？

最关键的决定因素是每季橄榄产量。正确的选择是在采摘季能达到70–100%负荷运转的设备，而非最便宜或功率最大的设备。

≤ 8万公斤/季
→ MM-250 或先考虑委托外部压榨
在此产量规模下，MM-250已是过剩配置。若客户已决定自建橄榄油厂：MM-250是正确选择。
8万–18万公斤/季
→ 推荐 MM-500
MM-500的最佳适配区间。产量充足，可维持接近满负荷运转。有良好的代加工服务潜力。效率与可管理性达到最佳平衡。
18万–34万公斤/季
→ MM-1000 或合作社模式
在此产量规模下，MM-1000具有明显的效率优势。联合周边橄榄种植户的合作社经营模式具有极强的商业吸引力。
产量未知 / 第一年
→ MM-500 作为稳妥默认选项
若产量不确定，MM-500提供最大灵活性：规模足以支撑专业化经营，可通过增加一台混合机扩容，还可提供代加工服务覆盖固定成本。
10 · 下一步行动计划

建议行动方案

A
确认橄榄产量
客户须提供每季预计橄榄产量（自有产量及任何第三方加工承诺）。这是确定正确设备规格的决定性因素。
B
场地评估
核实：可用建筑面积、三相电供应情况（及扩容成本）、供水条件以及拟安装地址的废水处理方案。
C
启动许可证申请
委托当地建筑师或工业工程师编制经营活动许可证申请材料。此项工作可与设备订购同步推进。
D
签订含CE条款的采购合同
确认设备规格后，正式采购合同须包含：完整生产线CE认证作为尾款支付前提条件、西班牙语技术手册，以及明确的调试完成日期。
E
安排进口资金
下单时须支付50%定金，余款在发货前支付。需统筹规划：进口关税、运抵现场的运输费用，以及安装调试期间到首次创收之间的资金周转。
F
提前采购配套设施
锅炉、储油罐、电气工程和灌装线须提前订购。这些本地采购项目有其自身交货周期，切勿等到设备到达后再行安排。
MEGANO MILLS 投资分析报告 · 编号：MM-OL-2026-01
编制：digitalAIventures LLC · 2026年4月 · 机密文件
供应商信息依协议保密 · 完整供应商档案可在签署保密协议后提供
所有财务预测均为基于公开市场数据和供应商规格参数的参考估算，不构成财务建议。实际结果因场地条件、市场价格和运营效率而存在差异。
